question: How does Excalidraw render and update canvas elements?

graph.answer:
Excalidraw splits rendering into two canvas layers and keeps the actual element data in a separate `Scene` store.

**Render path**
- `App` renders both canvases: `StaticCanvas` for the document content and `InteractiveCanvas` for overlays like selection, handles, and collaborators. See [App.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L2431).
- `StaticCanvas` is responsible for drawing the scene. It calls `renderStaticScene(...)`, which iterates `visibleElements`, calls `renderElement(...)` for each element, and also handles bound text, link icons, embeddables, frame clipping, and pending flowchart nodes. See [StaticCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/StaticCanvas.tsx#L22) and [staticScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/staticScene.ts#L300).
- `InteractiveCanvas` does not paint the base shapes. It prepares collaborator pointer data, then starts an `AnimationController` loop that repeatedly calls `renderInteractiveScene(...)`. That function draws selection UI, handles, highlights, snaps, remote cursors, and scrollbars. See [InteractiveCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/InteractiveCanvas.tsx#L86) and [interactiveScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/interactiveScene.ts#L1550).

**Update path**
- Most element/app changes go through `App.updateScene(...)`. It can update React state, replace all scene elements, or update collaborators. See [App.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L4717).
- Replacing the scene rebuilds the element maps and then calls `Scene.triggerUpdate()`. Mutating a single element also triggers an update if its version changed. See [Scene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/element/src/Scene.ts#L271), [Scene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/element/src/Scene.ts#L303), and [Scene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/element/src/Scene.ts#L427).
- `App` subscribes to `scene.onUpdate(this.triggerRender)`, so any scene invalidation forces a React update. `triggerRender()` either calls `scene.triggerUpdate()` or `setState({})` to make the canvases rerender. See [App.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L3235) and [App.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L4672).
- Both canvas components are wrapped in `React.memo`, so they only redraw when the relevant props actually change. `StaticCanvas` also supports RAF throttling for scene rendering. See [StaticCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/StaticCanvas.tsx#L94) and [InteractiveCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/InteractiveCanvas.tsx#L275).

**Practical summary**
- Base elements live in `Scene`.
- `Scene` changes invalidate the scene.
- `App` converts that invalidation into a React rerender.
- `StaticCanvas` repaints the actual drawing.
- `InteractiveCanvas` repaints the UI overlay continuously or when its tracked props change.

If you want, I can trace one concrete interaction next, like drag, resize, or text editing, through this pipeline.
